mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand and economic health, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong consumer spending supports economic growth
failure_condition: If RSAFS shows consistent growth but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such periods, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate below a critical threshold signals an overheating labor market, which often precedes inflationary pressures and tighter monetary policy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Historically low unemployment can precede economic slowdowns
failure_condition: If UNRATE falls below 6.0% and S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of subsequent instances, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows significant acceleration while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains exceptionally low, it indicates that monetary policy is behind the curve, which will necessitate aggressive tightening and negatively impact equity valuations over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:Central banks react to inflation with rate hikes
failure_condition: If the specified trigger conditions are met, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of cases, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts reflects strong investment and consumer confidence in the economy, indicating a robust demand environment that supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market strength is a leading indicator of economic health
failure_condition: If HOUST shows consistent upward trends but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such periods within an economic expansion, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in nonfarm payrolls signifies a healthy labor market and expanding economy, which directly supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon of one week.
evidence_refs: fact:Job growth drives consumer confidence and spending
failure_condition: If PAYEMS consistently increases month-over-month, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained contraction in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing activity and overall economic slowdown, which negatively impacts corporate profits and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month average growth rate falls below -0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key indicator of economic output
failure_condition: If INDPRO's 3-month average growth rate falls below -0.5%, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of subsequent periods, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of strong retail sales and a declining unemployment rate indicates robust consumer health and spending power, which drives corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer strength is vital for equity market performance
failure_condition: If RSAFS shows positive growth and UNRATE is declining, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of such periods, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate signals an aggressive monetary tightening cycle, which raises borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, increases discount rates, and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25% in 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Higher interest rates increase cost of capital
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25% in 3 months, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of subsequent instances, this hypothesis is falsified.
